content.problemStatement:
Dominater and Everule are very competitive, and keep trying to show that they are better at competitive programming than the other. What better measure is there than their rating?

Both of them participated in a contest. Before the contest, Dominater's rating was R1R_1R1​ and Everule's rating was R2R_2R2​. Dominater's rating changed by D1D_1D1​ in the contest, and Everule's rating changed by D2D_2D2​.

Who has the higher final rating after the contest?

Print "Dominater" if his rating is higher, and "Everule" if his rating is higher (without the quotes).

It is guaranteed they do not have equal ratings at the end of the contest.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain two space-separated integers R1R_1R1​ and R2R_2R2​, denoting the initial ratings of Dominater and Everule.
The second line of input will contain two space-separated integers D1D_1D1​ and D2D_2D2​, denoting the rating changes of Dominater and Everule.


Output Format
Output `Dominater` or `Everule`, depending on who has a higher rating at the end.


Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e, `Everule`, `EVERULE`, and `evERuLe` will all be treated as equivalent.
constraints: It is guaranteed that (R1 + D1) ≠ (R2 + D2) for every test case.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two space‑separated integers R1 and R2 — the initial ratings of Dominater and Everule. The second line contains two space‑separated integers D1 and D2 — the rating changes of Dominater and Everule in that contest.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing either "Dominater" if Dominater's final rating (R1+D1) is higher, or "Everule" otherwise. The case of the letters does not matter.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Final rating of Dominater = 1 + (-5) = -4. Final rating of Everule = 100 + (-150) = -50. Since -4 > -50, Dominater has the higher rating.
Dominater's final rating = 2636 + (-116) = 2520. Everule's final rating = 2536 + (-11) = 2525. Everule's rating is higher, so the answer is Everule.

Test case 1: ratings stay the same, 3000 > 2999 → Dominater. Test case 2: Dominater final = 1500+200 = 1700, Everule final = 1500+(-199) = 1301 → Dominater is higher, but the guarantee says they differ, so output is Dominater. (Correction: actually 1700 > 1301, so Dominater.)

solution.approach: For each test case compute the final rating of Dominater as R1 + D1 and the final rating of Everule as R2 + D2. Compare the two values. If the first is larger, print "Dominater"; otherwise print "Everule". The algorithm is O(1) per test case and uses O(1) extra memory.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
